query: "gas prices" OR "gasoline prices" OR "oil prices" OR petrol

articles.title: Oil rises as uncertainty mounts over US-Iran truce
articles.description:
Oil prices rose on Friday as the prospect of a lasting truce between the U.S. and Iran was clouded after peace talks in Switzerland were called off and as Israel stepped up attacks on Lebanon.
...

articles.title: ECB's Wunsch keeps July hike in play despite Iran deal
articles.description: The European Central Bank may raise interest rates one more time as soon as next month if it sees more evidence of euro zone inflation spreading beyond energy, ECB policymaker Pierre Wunsch told Reuters, even as the US-Iran deal dents oil prices.

articles.title: Oil prices rise as Lebanon fighting erupts and Hormuz traffic still slow
articles.description: Brent crude reverses slide after oil, LNG tankers cross critical waterway.

articles.title: Ranchi RSS Office Attack: All 3 Accused Linked To Pakistan-Based TTH Terror Outfit Sent Blast Video To Handlers As Proof
articles.description: The probe into the Ranchi RSS office petrol bomb attack has uncovered alleged links between the three arrested accused and the ISI-backed Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan (TTH) terror outfit, with sources claiming they were radicalised through handlers in Dubai and sent videos of the attack abroad.

articles.title: Sources say arrested men in RSS office attack linked to ISI-backed TTH
articles.description: All three men arrested for petrol bomb attack on a Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) office in Ranchi, Jharkhand, on Tuesday (June 16) were linked to the ISI-funded Tehreek-e-Taliban Hindustan and were radicalised in Dubai, sources said.

articles.title: Average gas price falls below $4 a gallon
articles.description: NEW YORK -- U.S. gas prices fell to just below $4 a gallon on average Thursday, bringing some relief to drivers who have seen soaring costs amid Washington's war with Iran.

articles.title: Rupee Opens Flat at 94.35/$, Strengthens On Oil Relief, Inflow Support
articles.description: The Indian rupee opened flat at 94.35 against the US dollar on June 19, supported by easing crude oil prices and improved foreign inflows. The currency has gained around 1.2% in recent sessions as Brent crude stays below $80 and dollar index strength moderates global currency movements

articles.title: Crude Oil Prices Edge Higher As Delay In JD Vance’s Switzerland Trip Postpones Next Round Of Iran Talks
articles.description: Crude oil prices traded slightly higher on Friday after US Vice President JD Vance delayed his planned visit to Switzerland, where he was expected to lead the next round of talks with Iran. Brent crude rose above $80 per barrel, while WTI crude gained 0.5 percent during Asian trading hours

articles.title: Three held under UAPA for petrol bomb attack on RSS office in Ranchi
articles.description: The police claimed that one of the accused men tried to flee from custody, and that he was shot in the leg in self-defence.

articles.title: Rupee Rises 20 Paise to 94.20 Against US Dollar in Early Trade
articles.description: The rupee rose 20 paise to 94.20 against the US dollar in early trade on optimism over India-US trade talks, supportive oil prices and improving foreign inflows, even as Sensex and Nifty slipped and FIIs sold over ₹1,025 crore in equities.
